key_insight: The paper extends Perron--Frobenius theory and eigenvector centrality from real nonnegative networks to complex-weighted networks, enabling a principled notion of node importance when edge weights are complex.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers and scientists a way to rank nodes in complex-valued systems arising in quantum, electromagnetic, circuit, chemistry, and learning applications.
method: Generalize PF-style conditions and connect several complex-matrix formulations.
Define eigenvector-based centralities for complex-weighted adjacency matrices.
Work through examples from electron transport, circuit analysis, mathematical chemistry, and communication networks.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical results plus computed centralities on multiple example networks, but does not describe a benchmark dataset or quantitative comparison.
watch_item: Complex eigenvectors can be phase-dependent and may require careful interpretation and normalization to make centrality scores meaningful.
paper_title: Generalizing Perron--Frobenius theory and eigenvector-based centralities to networks with complex edge weights
